sonnet_review.biasedText: Trump has given listeners no reason to believe him on this.
sonnet_review.reason: This is a reasonable detection. The statement is an editorial judgment in the author's voice rather than a neutral report that no evidence was found. While I didn't flag this exact sentence, it aligns with the overall editorial tone I identified elsewhere.

sonnet_review.biasedText: At the very least, though, people should treat his story with great skepticism.
sonnet_review.reason: This is a defensible detection. The author directly prescribes how readers should interpret the information, which goes beyond neutral reporting. I didn't flag this but upon review it is a clear instance of the author inserting opinion.

gpt_review.biasedText: Trump added more color to the story in a Thursday campaign speech in New Hampshire
gpt_review.reason: 'Added more color' does imply Trump was embellishing or elaborating in a way that subtly editorializes. This is a defensible detection I didn't independently flag.

gpt_review.biasedText: in each version of the dramatic story
gpt_review.reason: I flagged the same text as a Subjective Qualifying Adjective. The word 'dramatic' is an editorial characterization that frames the claims before analysis.

gpt_review.biasedText: But Trump has given listeners no reason to believe him on this.
gpt_review.reason: Like Sonnet's detection of the same text, this is a defensible finding. The statement is editorial judgment presented in the author's declarative voice.

gpt_review.biasedText: Representatives for two anti-immigration organizations told us they had not heard of anything that would corroborate any of Trump's story, as did three experts at organizations favorable toward immigration. CNN's own search did not produce any evidence. The website FactCheck.org also found nothing.
gpt_review.reason: The article actually does describe reaching out to Trump's campaign, a pro-Trump super PAC, and both anti-immigration and pro-immigration organizations. The reporter did try to find sources that might support the claim. The fact that none could is the finding, not evidence of slant in source selection.

gpt_review.biasedText: more than 30 hours later, nobody had.
gpt_review.reason: I flagged this same passage as Slant. The framing emphasizes the absence of evidence for maximum rhetorical effect rather than presenting it neutrally as one data point.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: In each version of the dramatic story
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the editorializing adjective 'dramatic' applied to Trump's story, which frames the claims as sensational before fact-checking begins.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Cheung sent us links to news articles that were not proof
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed this blunt editorial dismissal. A more neutral framing would acknowledge the campaign's intent while noting the articles didn't substantiate the specific claim.
Sonnet missed these editorial adjectives applied to Breitbart's reporting.
Sonnet missed the selective ideological labeling of Breitbart without similar labeling of other cited organizations.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: the campaign also failed to provide any evidence that South American countries are emptying mental health facilities to somehow send patients into the US
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the dismissive word 'somehow' which editorializes about the plausibility of the mechanism.
Sonnet missed the loaded word 'despot' as an editorial choice over more neutral alternatives.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: As a last resort, we posted Trump's quotes on Twitter and invited the public to try to find support for them. More than 30 hours later, nobody had.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the rhetorical framing of the article's conclusion for maximum persuasive effect.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: Cheung sent us links to news articles that were not proof
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed this blunt editorial dismissal of the campaign's response.
GPT missed the editorial adjectives characterizing Breitbart's reporting.
GPT missed the selective ideological labeling of Breitbart without equivalent labeling of other organizations cited.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: the campaign also failed to provide any evidence that South American countries are emptying mental health facilities to somehow send patients into the US
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed the dismissive 'somehow' which editorializes about plausibility.
GPT missed the loaded word 'despot' as an editorial choice.
